rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Powell¿s deposition testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is denied. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett worked with and/or around any OEM components of a Crane Co. or Jenkins valve so whether defendant is liable for Jenkins valves is irrelevant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants sustained their initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendants. In their opposition plaintiffs conceded that there is insufficient evidence to prevail at trial against Hanson Permanente. Defendants¿ objections to Mr. Powell¿s deposition testimony and to Kaiser Gypsum¿s interrogatory responses are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of the reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WEDDING et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant John Crane Inc.'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs¿ Second Amended Complaint For Personal Injuries And Loss Of Consortium
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is denied. Plaintiffs¿ complaint contains sufficient allegations to state a claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. rulings.Case Title: ELISABETH SMITH et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Deft. Federal-Mogul Asbestos Personal Injury Trust As Successor To Felt Products Manufacturing Company'S Motion To Transfer Venue And Stay Proceedings Pending Transfer
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Applied Industrial Tech¿s joinder in Federal-Mogul Asbestos Personal Injury Trust¿s motion to transfer venue to the County of Riverside and stay proceedings pending transfer is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELISABETH SMITH et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Transfer Venue And Stay Proceedings Pending Transfer

Plaintiffs do not oppose defendant¿s motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
